---
title: "Features"
sidebar: en
---

Établi Table is a lean, native client for a self-hosted SeaTable instance. It talks only to the server you point it at — there is no central service and no third party in between. The figures below are real screenshots of the v0.1.0 Android build; they show the screens you can reach before connecting a server. The data screens require a reachable SeaTable instance and are not faked here.

## Not connected — the home state

The home tab uses the suite's terminal-style breadcrumb and clearly states that no SeaTable server is configured. It points you to Settings — set the server and credentials there first. Nothing is fetched until you connect.

![Home — not connected, prompting you to configure a server in Settings.](../_screens/0.1.0/01-login.png){width=320}

## Settings — server & credentials

Settings is where you enter your SeaTable instance URL and credentials. Credentials live in the platform secure store (Android EncryptedSharedPreferences). Once connected, the app talks directly to your server — and only to that server.

![Settings — enter the SeaTable server URL and credentials.](../_screens/0.1.0/02-settings.png){width=320}

## Backend-dependent screens

The data screens — browsing bases, opening tables, viewing rows and editing individual cells — require a **reachable SeaTable instance and valid credentials**. Those screens are intentionally **not faked** here: they become available once you connect a server in Settings. A future capture against a test SeaTable instance will extend this page with the live data views.

## Where to get it

Établi Table is **under active development** and currently Android-only. Install the signed development APK from the v0.1.0 release:

- **Android (signed APK)** — [GitHub Releases · v0.1.0](https://github.com/etabli-dev/etabli-table/releases/tag/v0.1.0)
- App Store (iOS), Google Play and F-Droid — planned, not yet available.
